Package com.jcabi.http

Interface Response

All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultResponse, JacksonResponse, JsonResponse, JsoupResponse, RestResponse, WebLinkingResponse, XmlResponse
@Immutable public interface Response
RESTful response returned by Request.fetch().

You can get this response from one of implementations of Request: 

 Response response = new JdkRequest("https://www.google.com")
   .header("Accept", "text/html")
   .fetch();

Instances of this interface are immutable and thread-safe.

Since:
0.8
See Also:

    Get back to the request it's related to.
    byte[]
    binary()
    Raw body as an array of bytes.
    String
    body()
    Get body as a string, assuming it's UTF-8 (if there is something else that can't be translated into a UTF-8 string a runtime exception will be thrown).
    Map<String,List<String>>
    headers()
    Get a collection of all headers.
    String
    reason()
    Get status line reason phrase.
    int
    status()
    Get status of the response as a positive integer number.

  • Method Details

    • back

      Request back()
      Get back to the request it's related to.
      Returns:
      The request we're in

    • status

      int status()
      Get status of the response as a positive integer number.
      Returns:
      The status code

    • reason

      String reason()
      Get status line reason phrase.
      Returns:
      The status line reason phrase

    • headers

      Map<String,List<String>> headers()
      Get a collection of all headers.
      Returns:
      The headers

    • body

      String body()
      Get body as a string, assuming it's UTF-8 (if there is something else that can't be translated into a UTF-8 string a runtime exception will be thrown).

      DISCLAIMER: The only encoding supported here is UTF-8. If the body of response contains any chars that can't be used and should be replaced with a "replacement character", a RuntimeException will be thrown. If you need to use some other encodings, use binary() instead.

      Returns:
      The body, as a UTF-8 string

    • binary

      byte[] binary()
      Raw body as an array of bytes.
      Returns:
      The body, as a UTF-8 string

    • as

      <T extends Response> T as(Class<T> type)
      Convert it to another type, by encapsulation.
      Type Parameters:
      T - Type to use
      Parameters:
      type - Type to use
      Returns:
      New response
